message:
Fix virtual scrolling layout issues in static viewer

Added CSS and JavaScript improvements to fix virtual scrolling behavior:

CSS CHANGES (index.html):
- Added .message-row { min-height: 68px; } class
- Ensures consistent row height for Clusterize.js measurements
- Prevents layout shift when rows are recycled during virtual scrolling

WHY: Clusterize.js needs consistent row heights to calculate scroll positions
accurately. Without min-height, rows with shorter content collapse, causing
the virtual scroll window to miscalculate visible range and skip rows.

JAVASCRIPT CHANGES (viewer.js):
- Added setHeightFromViewport() function in initVirtualList()
- Calculates scroll container height from viewport dimensions
- Formula: height = max(240px, viewport_height - container_top - 12px)
- Applies concrete height to #virtual-message-list element

- Updated resize handler to call setHeightFromViewport() before refresh
- Ensures scroll container resizes correctly when window resizes

WHY HEIGHT CALCULATION:
The scroll container needs a concrete height (not flex: 1 or height: auto)
for Clusterize to work properly. Without it:
- Clusterize can't determine how many rows fit in viewport
- All rows render (defeating the purpose of virtual scrolling)
- Performance degrades with large message lists

The calculation uses getBoundingClientRect() to find where the container
starts relative to viewport, then allocates remaining space minus 12px padding.
Falls back to 240px minimum for small viewports or if calculation fails.

RESIZE BEHAVIOR:
On window resize, the height is recalculated before Clusterize.refresh(true)
is called. This ensures:
- Vertical resize (browser zoom, split screen) updates visible row count
- Clusterize re-measures and adjusts virtual scroll buffer
- Scroll position is preserved when possible

These changes improve virtual scrolling stability and ensure smooth rendering
with 1,000+ messages across different viewport sizes and zoom levels.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
